content.body: Ah yeah, nonsensical or at least the algorithm is too complicated for no reason, someone will propose some changes based on investigation done during Firefox implementation. I'll take a look at this particular issue.
I guess you already saw, but I also managed to get people to reply directly to your issue.
annevk: I think https://github.com/whatwg/html/pull/12345 and the test PR are ready to land, unless I'm missing something.
content.formatted_body: annevk: I think https://github.com/whatwg/html/pull/12345 and the test PR are ready to land, unless I'm missing something.

content.body: Jake Archibald: I did some review with AI and the first thing it notes is that you used Bikeshed syntax `{{DOMException}}` which I'm pretty sure doesn't work or did someone add that to Wattsi?
content.formatted_body: Jake Archibald: I did some review with AI and the first thing it notes is that you used Bikeshed syntax {{DOMException}} which I'm pretty sure doesn't work or did someone add that to Wattsi?

content.body: Needs to be `DOMException`, the {{DOMException}} is broken in the deploy preview (I missed it at first because the first time you use the right syntax)
content.formatted_body: Needs to be <code>DOMException</code>, the {{DOMException}} is broken in the deploy preview (I missed it at first because the first time you use the right syntax)

content.body: In the HTML spec, the latest merge added https://html.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-a-target. In the source for that line, the `<dfn>` around "target" has an `attribute` attribute, but not a `for` attribute. I think it's the only such in the spec. Is that intentional?
content.formatted_body: In the HTML spec, the latest merge added https://html.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-a-target. In the source for that line, the <dfn> around "target" has an attribute attribute, but not a for attribute. I think it's the only such in the spec. Is that intentional?

content.body: (More generally, is there a doc that explains all the markup attributes in the spec source?)
(The best I've found so far is https://github.com/whatwg/wattsi/blob/main/Syntax.md)
https://speced.github.io/bikeshed/ explains some of the dfn export attributes. I don't think we have other resources at the moment.

> <@jmdyck:matrix.org> In the HTML spec, the latest merge added https://html.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-a-target. In the source for that line, the `<dfn>` around "target" has an `attribute` attribute, but not a `for` attribute. I think it's the only such in the spec. Is that intentional?

Ah that maybe should have had a for to the mixin I guess. Not a deliberate choice on my part
content.formatted_body:
In reply to @jmdyck:matrix.org
In the HTML spec, the latest merge added https://html.spec.whatwg.org/#dom-a-target. In the source for that line, the <dfn> around "target" has an attribute attribute, but not a for attribute. I think it's the only such in the spec. Is that intentional?
Ah that maybe should have had a for to the mixin I guess. Not a deliberate choice on my part

content.body: https://speced.github.io/bikeshed/#dfn-for seems to indicate that a `<dfn attribute>` needs a `for=SomeInterface`.
content.formatted_body: https://speced.github.io/bikeshed/#dfn-for seems to indicate that a <dfn attribute> needs a for=SomeInterface.

content.body: We do try to avoid adding new attributes with - as part of the name, right?
that certainly seems to be the precedent, yeah
